**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Count all checkpoints when computing missing totals**

The doctor report undercounts missing checkpoints because it derives `missing_checkpoint_count` from `list_checkpoints(... limit: Some(500))`, and `list_checkpoints` enforces that cap. In environments with more than 500 transcript sources, missing files beyond the first page are silently ignored, so `ai doctor` can report a healthy/low missing count when many checkpoints are actually stale.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Make parse-error uniqueness robust for NULL previews**

The dedupe key for `transcript_parse_errors` uses `UNIQUE(source_id, line_no, error, record_preview)` while `record_preview` is nullable. In SQLite, `NULL` values do not collide under a unique constraint, so repeated `INSERT OR IGNORE` calls with `record_preview = NULL` create duplicate rows instead of being ignored. Since scanner paths record some parse errors with `None` previews (e.g., oversized records), retries can inflate parse-error counts and grow this table indefinitely.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.
